claim_under_attack: The stated requirements are internally inconsistent because final removals cannot both require human approval and reliably occur within 60 seconds when human review averages 4 hours.
evidence_or_reason: The claim treats the 4-hour average review time as a fixed operating constant rather than a current-state baseline. No evidence is cited that this latency is irreducible — it may reflect queue staffing, tooling gaps, or content mix that are all addressable. A PRD that declares the SLA unachievable without first scoping whether the review process itself can be redesigned (tiered queues, pre-screened categories, on-call escalation paths) is doing policy arbitration rather than product definition. The inconsistency is real only if review latency is treated as immutable, which side_b assumes but never defends.
